questions.q: What is the primary cause of the disease amoebiasis?
Describe the symptoms of tuberculosis. (4 marks)
questions.answer: Symptoms of tuberculosis include coughing, chest pain, coughing up blood, fatigue, weight loss, loss of appetite, fever, and night sweats.

questions.q: Which of the following diseases is caused by a virus? (1 mark)
What is the function of interferons in the human body?
questions.answer: Interferons are proteins that help to defend against viral infections by interfering with the replication of viruses.

questions.q: True or False: Vaccination can provide 100% protection against diseases.
Describe the role of B-cells and T-cells in the immune system. (5 marks)
questions.answer: B-cells produce antibodies to fight infections, while T-cells directly attack infected cells or produce chemical signals that activate the immune response.

questions.q: What is the name of the disease caused by the deficiency of insulin?
Which of the following is a non-communicable disease? (1 mark)
Explain the difference between active and passive immunity. (3 marks)
questions.answer: Active immunity is acquired through direct exposure to a pathogen, while passive immunity is acquired through the transfer of antibodies from another individual.

questions.q: What is the term for the process by which the body's immune system is able to recognize and respond to specific pathogens?
